# forktrust

> Safe-by-default git worktree manager for parallel AI coding agents. Refuse-on-conflict merges. Never-lose-WIP guarantee. Per-worktree port allocation. One command (`forktrust agent-docs`) to teach any AI how to use it.

Install: `brew install binkovsky/forktrust/forktrust`

## Core docs

- [README](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/README.md): full feature list, install, comparison table vs gtr/gwq/wtp/workz/claude-squad
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/AGENTS.md): how AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, Codex) should use the tool
- [LICENSE](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/LICENSE): MIT

## Workflow

The canonical loop, in three commands:

```bash
forktrust new <slug>      # create isolated worktree at .forktrust/worktrees/<slug>
ft <slug>                 # cd into the worktree (see "Shell integration" below)
# ... edit files there (NOT in main checkout) ...
forktrust finish <slug>   # commit WIP + merge to main + push + cleanup
```

To abandon without merging (WIP is snapshotted to `wip/<branch>-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<sha7>` on origin first):

```bash
forktrust rm <slug>
```

## Hard guarantees

- **Pre-flight refusal:** `finish` and `rm` run ALL refusal checks BEFORE any git mutation. Non-zero exit ⇒ no commit, merge, push, or branch delete happened. Side effects are never partial.
- **Dry-run matches reality:** `--dry-run --json` predicts the exact `would_refuse` and exit code of the real command.
- **Never-lose-WIP:** `rm` always pushes uncommitted/unpushed work to `wip/<branch>-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<sha7>` on origin before touching the local state. `<sha7>` is the WIP commit's short SHA, so same-second `rm` on different branches never collides.
- **Refuse-on-conflict:** `finish` aborts the merge on any conflict (exit 2). No `--strategy ours/theirs`, ever.
- **Refuse-on-ignored:** `rm`/`finish` refuse (exit 14) if the worktree has ignored files that `git worktree remove` would silently delete. `--force` skips.
- **.env.local ownership:** only treated as forktrust-managed if (a) regular file (not symlink) at the worktree root and (b) starts with the exact `# Managed by forktrust...` header line. User .env.local with any other content is preserved.

## Shell integration

```bash
# ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc
ft() {
  local p
  p="$(forktrust cd "$1" 2>/dev/null)" || { echo "forktrust: no worktree '$1'" >&2; return 1; }
  cd "$p" || return 1
}
```

Or: `forktrust shell <slug>` opens an interactive shell in the worktree (with `FORKTRUST_SLUG` exported).

## .forktrustconfig (per-repo, committed)

```toml
[ports]
range = "3000-3099"
size  = 10
vars  = ["PORT", "NEXT_PUBLIC_PORT"]

[[hooks.post_create]]
type = "copy"
from = ".env"
to   = ".env"

[[hooks.post_create]]
type = "symlink"
from = "node_modules"
to   = "node_modules"

[[hooks.post_create]]
type = "command"
run  = "pnpm install"   # requires `forktrust trust`
```

## Stable exit codes (will not change across releases)

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| 0 | success |
| 2 | merge conflict (refuse to auto-resolve) |
| 3 | main worktree is dirty |
| 4 | push failed |
| 5 | wip/* push failed (worktree NOT removed) |
| 6 | no worktree matching slug |
| 7 | slug matches multiple projects |
| 8 | hook failed or untrusted command hook |
| 9 | no origin remote configured |
| 10 | main checkout on wrong branch |
| 11 | cwd is in unregistered git repo |
| 12 | rm/finish could not determine ahead count |
| 13 | rm: worktree removed but branch -D failed |
| 14 | rm/finish refused: ignored files in worktree would be permanently deleted |

- [Homebrew tap](https://github.com/binkovsky/homebrew-forktrust)
